creator: dewayne@heuristicsystems.com.au
text:
(In reply to Bernard Spil from comment #3)
Thanks Bernard,
The patch applied cleanly, the build completed and ran as expected.

OpenSSL 1.1.0e  16 Feb 2017
dd if=/dev/zero bs=8m count=64 | openssl enc -e -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:fred | openssl enc -d -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:fred | dd of=/dev/null
64+0 records in
64+0 records out
536870912 bytes transferred in 1.985984 secs (270329891 bytes/sec)
1048576+0 records in
1048576+0 records out
536870912 bytes transferred in 1.986184 secs (270302705 bytes/sec)

The patch is good for inclusion into security/openssl-devel/files/ 

--=--

Though, I was surprised to note that 
# openssl version; dd if=/dev/zero bs=8m count=64 | openssl enc -e -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:fred | openssl enc -d -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:fred | dd of=/dev/null
LibreSSL 2.4.5
64+0 records in
64+0 records out
536870912 bytes transferred in 1.665894 secs (322271914 bytes/sec)
1048576+0 records in
1048576+0 records out
536870912 bytes transferred in 1.665990 secs (322253367 bytes/sec)

( Aside: Rhetorical question - should I adjust the buffer size to better match my non-default vfs.read_max=128 during disk reads? ;) )
